MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication On Behalf Of Dignity Health
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sheryl A. Traum, esq., telephone 510-325-8321, appearing for Plaintiff;
Mark Wilson, Esq., Manning & Kas ET AL, Telephone 415-400-0829, appearing for Defendant;

Ruling - A R G U E D No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication On Behalf Of Dignity Health

Defendant Dignity Health's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied. Defendant does not maintain its initial burden of production. Defendant fails to show that the fine dust on the light colored floor was a trivial defect and therefore there is a triable issue of material fact regarding the existence of a dangerous condition of property. (Vargas Special Rog. Response, #1.) Privette v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and its progeny are inapposite. The instant action does not involve the injury of an employee of a contractor. This case does not implicate the fairness/worker's compensation issues of Privette. Dignity fails to show that it did not create the dangerous condition. (See Getchell v. Rogers Jewelry (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 381, 385.) In fact, Dignity performed HVAC/plumbing work in the bathroom shortly before the accident. (Traum Dec., Ex. 3., Mony Depo., 107:3-109:20.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

*After hearing an order is signed*
